seo.title: Building Data Extraction with Llama Stack + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with Llama Stack + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Llama Stack provides a standardized Python interface for building robust Llama-powered agents with built-in safety, memory, and tool capabilities, making it ideal for data extraction from unstructured documents. Data extraction use cases demand precise schema compliance and field accuracy, where even small errors can cascade through downstream systems. Vex guardrails wrap agent responses to verify schema adherence, completeness, and data integrity, preventing costly extraction failures.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with Llama Stack
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Llama Stack data extraction pipeline starts with document ingestion into the memory bank, followed by schema-guided agent calls that leverage Llama 3.2 models for structured extraction. The agent uses the LlamaStackClient to orchestrate turns between reasoning, tool calls (like document retrieval), and final JSON output generation. Safety shields filter responses while memory retrieval provides context from prior extractions.

The core flow involves: 1) Loading documents into memory, 2) Creating extraction schemas with Pydantic models, 3) Running agentic loops with turn limits, and 4) Post-processing outputs for validation. This architecture handles complex documents like invoices or contracts by breaking extraction into multi-turn reasoning paths.

Vex integration occurs post-agent as a verification layer, checking extracted JSON against expected schemas before persisting results.
architectureOverview.diagram:
┌─────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────┐
│   Unstructured  │───▶│ LlamaStackClient │───▶│   Vex Verify    │
│     Documents   │    │ - Memory Bank    │    │ - Schema Check  │
└─────────────────┘    │ - Safety Shield  │    │ - Field Accuracy│
                       │ - Agent Loop     │    └───────────────┘
                       │ - Tool Calls     │           │
                       └──────────────────┘           ▼
                                                      ┌─────────────────┐
                                                      │ Validated JSON  │
                                                      │   Extraction    │
                                                      └─────────────────┘

risks.name: Safety shield filtering producing false positives on benign content
risks.description: Llama Stack's safety filters may block legitimate extraction outputs containing financial terms or addresses mistaken for sensitive data
risks.mitigation: Configure Vex custom safety rules that whitelist extraction schemas and validate business context before filtering

risks.name: Agentic loop turn limits exceeded without meaningful resolution
risks.description: Complex documents exhaust turn limits during iterative extraction refinement, producing incomplete schemas
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors loop progress and validates partial outputs against completeness criteria, forcing early termination with error signals

risks.name: Tool runtime responses not conforming to expected schema
risks.description: Document retrieval tools return malformed JSON or missing fields expected by the extraction agent
risks.mitigation: Vex schema enforcement wraps all tool responses, rejecting non-conformant outputs before agent processing

risks.name: Memory bank retrieval hallucinating document associations
risks.description: Agent incorrectly associates extracted fields with wrong documents due to fuzzy memory retrieval
risks.mitigation: Vex validates document IDs and metadata consistency between memory context and extraction output

implementation.title: Initialize Llama Stack Client
implementation.description: Set up the LlamaStackClient with memory bank and safety shield for document processing
implementation.code:
from llama_stack_client import LlamaStackClient
from llama_stack_client.types import ModelConfig

client = LlamaStackClient(
    model_config=ModelConfig(model="llama-3.2-11b-vision-instruct"),
    memory_bank=True,
    safety_shield=True
)

# Load document into memory
client.memory.store(
    doc_id="invoice_123",
    content="Invoice #123 from Acme Corp...",
    metadata={"type": "invoice"}
)

implementation.title: Define Extraction Schema with Pydantic
implementation.description: Create structured output schema for invoice data extraction using Pydantic models
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional
from llama_stack_client.types import ToolSchema

class InvoiceData(BaseModel):
    invoice_number: str = Field(..., description="Unique invoice ID")
    date: str = Field(..., description="Invoice date in YYYY-MM-DD")
    total_amount: float = Field(..., description="Total invoice amount")
    vendor_name: str = Field(..., description="Vendor/Supplier name")
    line_items: list[dict] = Field(default_factory=list)

extraction_schema = ToolSchema(
    name="extract_invoice",
    pydantic_model=InvoiceData,
    description="Extract structured invoice data"
)

implementation.description: Execute schema-guided extraction with turn limits and tool integration

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Agent Response with Schema Verification
vexIntegration.description: Use Vex to validate extraction output against Pydantic schema post-agent
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

# After llama stack agent response
raw_extraction = response.tool_calls[0].result

verified = vex.verify(
    data=raw_extraction,
    checks=[
        vex.schema(InvoiceData),
        vex.field_accuracy([
            vex.field("invoice_number").regex(r'^INV-\d{6}$'),
            vex.field("total_amount").gt(0)
        ])
    ]
)

if verified.valid:
    save_extraction(verified.data)
else:
    logger.error(f"Extraction failed: {verified.errors}")

vexIntegration.title: Complete Extraction with Field Completeness Check
vexIntegration.description: Verify all required fields present and validate business rules
vexIntegration.code:
verified = vex.verify(
    data=extracted_data,
    checks=[
        vex.completeness([
            "invoice_number",
            "date",
            "total_amount",
            "vendor_name"
        ]),
        vex.type_consistency({
            "total_amount": "float",
            "date": "date"
        }),
        vex.custom(lambda data: len(data.line_items) > 0, "Must have line items")
    ],
    risk_profile="medium"
)

assert verified.valid, f"Incomplete extraction: {verified.errors}"
